tasks.objective: Create deterministic Go CLI skeleton, command-result JSON envelope, exit code constants, config discovery, and unit tests.
acceptance_checks: CLI exposes stable command-result envelope shape.
Exit-code constants match docs/product/prd.md.
Go module path is github.com/Clyra-AI/lumyn and Go version is pinned to 1.26.4.
T1 has no external runtime dependencies unless explicitly justified and pinned.
Tests pass without network, sandbox credentials, or model keys.
Unknown commands fail closed with exit code 2 while still emitting the command-result JSON envelope.
FDN-001: Create the Go CLI skeleton, command-result JSON envelope, exit-code constants, command dispatch, and repo-local validation baseline needed before product commands are implemented.
FR14: FR14: Stable CLI Output - Lumyn must support stable exit codes and `--json`.
NFR9: NFR9: Debuggable Errors - Errors must include file, path, command, object, or line references where possible.

runtime.dependency_policy: standard library only for T1; add pinned dependencies only when required by later tasks

tasks.scope_note: T1 includes alignment drift correction from Python placeholder to pinned Go CLI runtime before implementation continues.

runtime_pins.dependency_policy: standard library first; pinned Go modules only when a task proves need and records justification
runtime_pins.live_work_policy: blocked until deterministic replay foundation passes and human approval unlocks sandbox/model/network posture
runtime_pins.provider_policy: OpenAI-compatible HTTP including custom base_url local endpoints, and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters; no model key or network in deterministic bootstrap
runtime_pins.artifact_namespace: .factory/artifacts/ for Factory evidence; product artifacts use workflows/, cassettes/, runs/, baselines/, schemas/, and examples/

slice_rationale.why_this_shape: T1 is scoped to create deterministic Go CLI skeleton, command-result JSON envelope, exit code constants, config discovery, and unit tests.
slice_rationale.vertical_slice_preference: Vertical slices are preferred; foundation-only work must justify why downstream product slices need the shared contract first.

changelog.draft_entry: Initialize Lumyn Go CLI skeleton, command-result envelope, exit-code constants, config discovery, and local validation commands.

tasks.contract_impact: Introduces the public CLI shell, command-result JSON envelope, exit-code constants, and config discovery contract.
tasks.versioning_migration_impact: Pre-1.0 MVP work may evolve contracts, but introduced schema_version values, exit codes, artifact names, and command-result fields must retain explicit migration notes before release.
architecture_constraints: Preserve Go 1.26.4 and module path github.com/Clyra-AI/lumyn unless alignment artifacts are updated first.
Keep deterministic bootstrap work free of live network calls, sandbox credentials, and model keys.
Keep command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and persisted artifacts repo-relative and schema-backed where a schema exists.
Fail closed when proof strength, redaction confidence, credential posture, stale cassette state, or declared controls are ambiguous.
Record state owner, feedback source, deletion/blast-radius impact, rollback path, and evidence refs for medium/high risk architecture changes.
tasks.adr_trigger_reason: Required because this task changes public, executable, CLI, schema, artifact, CI/review, redaction/share, live/eval, or proof semantics captured by the Lumyn plan.
tdd_first_failing_tests: Add or update the smallest failing test, fixture, or schema validation for T1 acceptance before implementation when practical.
If a red-first check is not practical, record test_first_skipped_reason in the validation report with compensating evidence.

cost_perf_impact.measurement_expectation: No measurable runtime or cost increase expected beyond normal local CLI validation.
cost_perf_impact.budget_trigger: Add explicit performance/cost evidence if implementation introduces repeated replay, live network, model calls, generated artifacts, or large fixture processing.

chaos_failure_hypothesis.hypothesis: Unknown commands, invalid config paths, and write failures must still emit command-result JSON and fail closed.
chaos_failure_hypothesis.expected_fail_closed_behavior: Return structured command-result/error evidence, do not mark acceptance green, and update validation/scope closure with the failure or blocker.

semantic_invariants: Every command path emits the stable command-result envelope, including failures.
Exit codes remain aligned with docs/product/prd.md and tests.
Validation evidence records command, exit status, and skipped-command reason when applicable.
Factory evidence and product artifacts use repo-relative paths only.
No task may claim closure for PRD scope that remains missing, blocked, or deferred without explicit evidence.
tasks.plan_drift_policy: If this task changes runtime, distribution, provider, dependency, artifact namespace, live-work, guide, validation-contract, acceptance-ledger, acceptance-mapping, or scope-closure decisions, update context brief, execution plan, task packets, validation contract, acceptance ledger, acceptance mapping, and scope-closure map before implementation continues.

engineering_policy_refs.rule: Behavior, flags, output shape, exit codes, artifact paths, install paths, and workflow semantics update docs and Factory planning artifacts together.
Command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and product artifacts are public or executable contracts.
Release-sensitive work defines version, changelog, install, artifact-integrity, and UAT evidence before merge.
Validation, work-proof, PR lifecycle, scanner, and scope-closure evidence use repo-relative refs.

CI/workflow/policy/lifecycle work preserves required-check metadata, owner-review coverage, workflow permissions, concurrency, timeouts, toolchain-pin setup, action-ref posture, and branch-protection evidence or approved exceptions.
Structured data is read through parsers, schemas, stable APIs, or approved exceptions.
CLI tasks define JSON, non-interactive, quiet, compact, typed-exit, and machine-readable error behavior before agent-facing use.
Large output is cited by artifact ref with full-output hash and truncation metadata.
Shareable artifacts must declare redaction posture and recursively redact sensitive data.

architecture_guidance_refs.rule: Medium/high risk tasks record state owner, feedback source, blast radius, source-of-truth impact, and rollback/deletion checks.
Behavior and schema/output changes use test-first evidence or a structured skipped reason.
Boundary, public contract, failure semantics, credential/network, release, or major reliability/perf changes require ADR or decision note.
Fan-out, repeated runs, or model calls declare expected cost/performance impact.
Retry, cleanup, persistence, filesystem mutation, redaction, live network, and provider code require hardening/failure-mode evidence.
Deterministic bootstrap has no network/secrets; live work requires approval and evidence.

deprecated_worker_aliases.migration_behavior: block active task packets until required_worker_chain is migrated

scope_exclusions: training shared models on private artifacts

factoryd_runtime.approval_posture: human approval required for live credentials, high-risk tasks, and merge
factoryd_runtime.credential_posture: no ambient secrets during deterministic MVP bootstrap
factoryd_runtime.network_posture: offline by default until live sandbox/model work is approved

Keep deterministic bootstrap work free of live network calls, sandbox credentials, and model keys.
Keep command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and persisted artifacts repo-relative and schema-backed where a schema exists.
Fail closed when proof strength, redaction confidence, credential posture, stale cassette state, or declared controls are ambiguous.
Record state owner, feedback source, deletion/blast-radius impact, rollback path, and evidence refs for medium/high risk architecture changes.
tasks.adr_trigger_reason: Required because this task changes public, executable, CLI, schema, artifact, CI/review, redaction/share, live/eval, or proof semantics captured by the Lumyn plan.
tdd_first_failing_tests: Add or update the smallest failing test, fixture, or schema validation for T5 acceptance before implementation when practical.
If a red-first check is not practical, record test_first_skipped_reason in the validation report with compensating evidence.

cost_perf_impact.measurement_expectation: Keep deterministic commands fast and local; live/eval work must record duration, retry, and cost evidence before closure.
cost_perf_impact.budget_trigger: Add explicit performance/cost evidence if implementation introduces repeated replay, live network, model calls, generated artifacts, or large fixture processing.

chaos_failure_hypothesis.hypothesis: Network access, stale cassette data, or proof gaps must fail replay verification rather than reporting green.
chaos_failure_hypothesis.expected_fail_closed_behavior: Return structured command-result/error evidence, do not mark acceptance green, and update validation/scope closure with the failure or blocker.

semantic_invariants: Every command path emits the stable command-result envelope, including failures.
Exit codes remain aligned with docs/product/prd.md and tests.
Validation evidence records command, exit status, and skipped-command reason when applicable.
Factory evidence and product artifacts use repo-relative paths only.
No task may claim closure for PRD scope that remains missing, blocked, or deferred without explicit evidence.
Schema-backed artifacts must validate against the declared schema version or fail closure.
tasks.plan_drift_policy: If this task changes runtime, distribution, provider, dependency, artifact namespace, live-work, guide, validation-contract, acceptance-ledger, acceptance-mapping, or scope-closure decisions, update context brief, execution plan, task packets, validation contract, acceptance ledger, acceptance mapping, and scope-closure map before implementation continues.

deprecated_worker_aliases.migration_behavior: block active task packets until required_worker_chain is migrated

scope_exclusions: training shared models on private artifacts

factoryd_runtime.approval_posture: human approval required for live credentials, high-risk tasks, and merge
factoryd_runtime.credential_posture: no ambient secrets during deterministic MVP bootstrap
factoryd_runtime.network_posture: offline by default until live sandbox/model work is approved

changed path outside allowed_paths
forbidden path changed
required validation command failed
plan drift detected

redaction_posture.redaction_notes: Recursive redaction is required before customer-safe/public sharing; task-run evidence remains internal unless explicitly exported.
redaction_posture.recursive_policy: nested owner, credential, secret, endpoint, and machine-local path fields must be redacted before sharing

agent_native_cli_checks: Agent-facing CLI output supports stable JSON mode or records an approved deferral.

tasks.objective: Implement local trace/report rendering and `lumyn demo` with bundled reports. Split: Local Reports And Bundled Demo Reports.
acceptance_checks: Report first screen shows workflow, result, proof strength, why, evidence, fix target, and next command.
`lumyn demo` opens bundled reports without model key, sandbox, or hosted account.
RCRR-008: Local HTML report identifies the fix target.
RCRR-010: Bundled static traces open without model key, sandbox, or hosted account.
RCRR-011: `lumyn demo` opens the bundled agent-failure and proof-gap reports with no install, model key, sandbox, or hosted account.
PULL-005: User fixes docs, OpenAPI descriptions, auth guidance, endpoint ambiguity, or read-back gaps based on Lumyn output.
FR2: FR2: Local-First Operation - The MVP must work without a hosted Lumyn account.
FR13: FR13: Local Trace And Report - Lumyn must produce a local JSON trace and HTML report.
FR25: FR25: Portable Artifacts - Workflow contracts, cassettes, traces, reports, and baselines must remain useful outside a hosted service.
NFR1: NFR1: Fast First Value - A technical user should run `init`, `check`, and open bundled traces in under 10 minutes.
NFR4: NFR4: Proof Honesty - Reports must clearly distinguish confirmed read-back proof, trace-only evidence, and proof gaps.
NFR6: NFR6: Local Privacy - Lumyn must not send specs, docs, traces, cassettes, or API responses to Lumyn by default.
NFR9: NFR9: Debuggable Errors - Errors must include file, path, command, object, or line references where possible.
NFR14: NFR14: No Dashboard Dependency - The MVP must explain value through local CLI output and local reports.
NFR12: NFR12: Artifact Stability - Schemas and artifacts must be versioned.
RCRR-012: `lumyn demo` includes bundled proof-gap and boundary-violation/safety finding examples without requiring a hosted account, model key, or sandbox.

architecture_guidance_refs.rule: Medium/high risk tasks record state owner, feedback source, blast radius, source-of-truth impact, and rollback/deletion checks.
Behavior and schema/output changes use test-first evidence or a structured skipped reason.
Boundary, public contract, failure semantics, credential/network, release, or major reliability/perf changes require ADR or decision note.
Fan-out, repeated runs, or model calls declare expected cost/performance impact.
Retry, cleanup, persistence, filesystem mutation, redaction, live network, and provider code require hardening/failure-mode evidence.
Deterministic bootstrap has no network/secrets; live work requires approval and evidence.

engineering_policy_refs.rule: Behavior, flags, output shape, exit codes, artifact paths, install paths, and workflow semantics update docs and Factory planning artifacts together.
Command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and product artifacts are public or executable contracts.
Release-sensitive work defines version, changelog, install, artifact-integrity, and UAT evidence before merge.
Validation, work-proof, PR lifecycle, scanner, and scope-closure evidence use repo-relative refs.

CI/workflow/policy/lifecycle work preserves required-check metadata, owner-review coverage, workflow permissions, concurrency, timeouts, toolchain-pin setup, action-ref posture, and branch-protection evidence or approved exceptions.
Structured data is read through parsers, schemas, stable APIs, or approved exceptions.
CLI tasks define JSON, non-interactive, quiet, compact, typed-exit, and machine-readable error behavior before agent-facing use.
Large output is cited by artifact ref with full-output hash and truncation metadata.
Shareable artifacts must declare redaction posture and recursively redact sensitive data.

tasks.objective: Implement action-boundary object and deterministic boundary checks.
acceptance_checks: Final-state pass plus forbidden-path violation fails the workflow.
Unenforced declared controls never report as passed.
LVCIS-005: A forbidden path call fails the workflow even when final state passes.
LVCIS-006: Request and write-operation budgets are enforced.
FR17: FR17: Action Boundary - Lumyn must support a first-class action-boundary object and deterministic boundary checks.
FR18: FR18: Boundary Failure Semantics - Lumyn must fail a workflow when expected final state passes but configured action boundaries are violated.
FR9: FR9: Canonical Evidence Schema - Record, replay verify, live verify, eval, validators, and reports must consume canonical evidence events.
FR14: FR14: Stable CLI Output - Lumyn must support stable exit codes and `--json`.
NFR9: NFR9: Debuggable Errors - Errors must include file, path, command, object, or line references where possible.
NFR12: NFR12: Artifact Stability - Schemas and artifacts must be versioned.
LVCIS-009: Action-boundary violations that indicate scope escalation, data exposure risk, or out-of-policy action are labeled safety/security-relevant and fail even when final state passes.

architecture_guidance_refs.rule: Medium/high risk tasks record state owner, feedback source, blast radius, source-of-truth impact, and rollback/deletion checks.
Behavior and schema/output changes use test-first evidence or a structured skipped reason.
Boundary, public contract, failure semantics, credential/network, release, or major reliability/perf changes require ADR or decision note.
Fan-out, repeated runs, or model calls declare expected cost/performance impact.
Retry, cleanup, persistence, filesystem mutation, redaction, live network, and provider code require hardening/failure-mode evidence.
Deterministic bootstrap has no network/secrets; live work requires approval and evidence.

engineering_policy_refs.rule: Behavior, flags, output shape, exit codes, artifact paths, install paths, and workflow semantics update docs and Factory planning artifacts together.
Command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and product artifacts are public or executable contracts.
Release-sensitive work defines version, changelog, install, artifact-integrity, and UAT evidence before merge.
Validation, work-proof, PR lifecycle, scanner, and scope-closure evidence use repo-relative refs.

CI/workflow/policy/lifecycle work preserves required-check metadata, owner-review coverage, workflow permissions, concurrency, timeouts, toolchain-pin setup, action-ref posture, and branch-protection evidence or approved exceptions.
Structured data is read through parsers, schemas, stable APIs, or approved exceptions.
CLI tasks define JSON, non-interactive, quiet, compact, typed-exit, and machine-readable error behavior before agent-facing use.
Large output is cited by artifact ref with full-output hash and truncation metadata.
Shareable artifacts must declare redaction posture and recursively redact sensitive data.

changelog.draft_entry: Add eval baseline comparison, drift labeling, cost/duration reporting, and diagnostic failure classification.

tasks.contract_impact: Adds baseline comparison, drift labels, cost/duration, and diagnostic failure classification contracts. It also preserves normalized local eval failure-event stability across baselines and drift diagnostics.
tasks.versioning_migration_impact: Pre-1.0 MVP work may evolve contracts, but introduced schema_version values, exit codes, artifact names, and command-result fields must retain explicit migration notes before release.
architecture_constraints: Preserve Go 1.26.4 and module path github.com/Clyra-AI/lumyn unless alignment artifacts are updated first.
Keep deterministic bootstrap work free of live network calls, sandbox credentials, and model keys.
Keep command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and persisted artifacts repo-relative and schema-backed where a schema exists.
Fail closed when proof strength, redaction confidence, credential posture, stale cassette state, or declared controls are ambiguous.
Record state owner, feedback source, deletion/blast-radius impact, rollback path, and evidence refs for medium/high risk architecture changes.
Safety/corpus-ready evidence must remain local-first, schema-backed, and corpus_eligible false by default unless an explicit future opt-in scope change is approved.
tasks.adr_trigger_reason: Required because this task changes public, executable, CLI, schema, artifact, CI/review, redaction/share, live/eval, or proof semantics captured by the Lumyn plan.
tdd_first_failing_tests: Add or update the smallest failing test, fixture, or schema validation for T12 acceptance before implementation when practical.
If a red-first check is not practical, record test_first_skipped_reason in the validation report with compensating evidence.

cost_perf_impact.measurement_expectation: Keep deterministic commands fast and local; live/eval work must record duration, retry, and cost evidence before closure.
cost_perf_impact.budget_trigger: Add explicit performance/cost evidence if implementation introduces repeated replay, live network, model calls, generated artifacts, or large fixture processing.

chaos_failure_hypothesis.hypothesis: Baseline drift, model snapshot uncertainty, high cost, or missing trace evidence must be classified rather than hidden as pass/fail noise.
chaos_failure_hypothesis.expected_fail_closed_behavior: Return structured command-result/error evidence, do not mark acceptance green, and update validation/scope closure with the failure or blocker.

semantic_invariants: Every command path emits the stable command-result envelope, including failures.
Exit codes remain aligned with docs/product/prd.md and tests.
Validation evidence records command, exit status, and skipped-command reason when applicable.
Factory evidence and product artifacts use repo-relative paths only.
No task may claim closure for PRD scope that remains missing, blocked, or deferred without explicit evidence.
Schema-backed artifacts must validate against the declared schema version or fail closure.
Eval baseline and diagnostic failure records must keep normalized failure fields stable across repeated runs.
tasks.plan_drift_policy: If this task changes runtime, distribution, provider, dependency, artifact namespace, live-work, guide, validation-contract, acceptance-ledger, acceptance-mapping, or scope-closure decisions, update context brief, execution plan, task packets, validation contract, acceptance ledger, acceptance mapping, and scope-closure map before implementation continues.

deprecated_worker_aliases.migration_behavior: block active task packets until required_worker_chain is migrated

scope_exclusions: training shared models on private artifacts

factoryd_runtime.approval_posture: human approval required for live credentials, high-risk tasks, and merge
factoryd_runtime.credential_posture: no ambient secrets during deterministic MVP bootstrap
factoryd_runtime.network_posture: offline by default until live sandbox/model work is approved

stop_conditions: missing runner-ready task contract
changed path outside allowed_paths
forbidden path changed
required validation command failed
plan drift detected
required lifecycle evidence missing without approved exception
credential or network access required before approval posture is unlocked
coverage policy evidence is missing or below threshold without an approved scoped exception
required-check metadata, workflow permissions, concurrency, timeouts, owner-review coverage, action-ref posture, or branch-protection evidence is missing for CI/workflow/policy/lifecycle work
acceptance ledger item missing, unmapped, or closed only at group level

coverage_policy_refs.exception_policy: coverage misses block promotion unless the task records an approved scoped exception with owner, reason, expiry or follow-up task, and compensating validation evidence

gated_by_acceptance_items.reason: Live eval work must not start until product pull shows a user recorded more than one workflow.
Live eval work must not start until product pull shows a user asked to run live eval before an API/docs release.

safety_corpus_ready_evidence_refs.rule: Eval baselines and drift diagnostics preserve normalized failure fields across repeated runs.
Failure classification remains corpus-ready without hosted upload.

changelog_intent.reason: Task may affect public CLI, artifact, docs, schema, or delivery behavior; record no-op rationale when no user-facing change occurs.

tasks.versioning_impact: Pre-1.0 MVP work; public output, schema, artifact, CLI, or install behavior changes must record migration notes before release.
tasks.migration_impact: None expected unless this task changes public outputs, schemas, artifacts, install/distribution behavior, or persisted evidence.

redaction_posture.redaction_notes: Recursive redaction is required before customer-safe/public sharing; task-run evidence remains internal unless explicitly exported.
redaction_posture.recursive_policy: nested owner, credential, secret, endpoint, and machine-local path fields must be redacted before sharing

agent_native_cli_checks: Agent-facing CLI output supports stable JSON mode or records an approved deferral.
Non-interactive or piped output is machine-readable unless the command is explicitly human-only.
Quiet and compact modes preserve status, evidence refs, typed exits, and machine-readable errors.

proof_of_behavior_requirements.rule: Tasks must close at or above their required_proof_level; workflow or user-visible closure requires proof-of-behavior scorecard evidence or approved exception.
